title: Reducing Generation Costs
concept: Prompt caching, shorter context via compression, and routing simple queries to cheaper models.

exercises.prompt: In the last lesson, we optimized embedding costs. Which technique helps reduce the overhead of sending many individual embedding requests?

options.text: Batching multiple texts into a single request
Using the most expensive generation model
Increasing the prompt length with extra examples

exercises.explain: Batching embedding calls allows you to process multiple texts in a single request, which is a key way to optimize embedding workflows as learned in the last lesson.

cards.title: The Cost of Text
cards.body: Large Language Models charge you for every single token they read and write. If you send the same massive system prompt over and over, you're throwing money away on repetitive work.

cards.title: Generation Optimization
cards.body: Reducing generation costs means using smart techniques like prompt caching, compressing context, and routing easier tasks to smaller, cheaper models so you don't pay for processing power you don't need.

cards.title: The Coffee Pre-Order
cards.body: Imagine prompt caching is like a coffee shop keeping your favorite daily order pre-made and waiting for you. Instead of brewing it from scratch every time you ask, they just hand it over instantly, saving time and expensive ingredients.

cards.title: Caching and Shrinking in Action
cards.body: A customer service bot reads a long chat history. By summarizing the older messages (compressing them) and caching the static system instructions, the bot processes 80% fewer active tokens, drastically lowering the bill.

cards.title: The Smartest Model Myth
cards.body: Many believe you must use the absolute smartest, most expensive model for every single user request. In reality, up to 70% of user queries are simple enough for a model that costs 10x less.

cards.title: Routing Efficiency
cards.body: Compare how we handle incoming queries to optimize costs versus brute-forcing everything.

left.text: Simple greeting -> cheap model. Complex logic -> flagship model. You pay only for what you use.

right.text: Every single 'hello' and typo goes to the most expensive flagship model. High bills.

exercises.prompt: How does compressing long conversation histories help reduce LLM generation costs?

options.text: It increases the latency of the model's response.
It reduces the number of tokens the model has to process.
It forces the model to use more expensive hardware.
It changes the embedding dimension of the output.

exercises.explain: Compressing or summarizing older messages directly reduces the number of tokens sent to the model, which lowers the overall cost of the API call.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Every single prompt in a application must be sent to the most powerful model to guarantee a good user experience.
exercises.explain: Not all queries need the most advanced model. Simple tasks like routing, classification, or basic Q&A can be handled by smaller, cheaper models to save money.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the cost-saving strategy:
exercises.sentence: To avoid paying to process the exact same system instructions on every request, we can use prompt caching.
exercises.explain: Prompt caching allows the LLM provider to reuse previously processed text blocks, significantly cutting down the cost of processing static instructions.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following designs represents an optimized AI routing system?

options.text: We hardcode all requests to go to the most expensive model to ensure the highest quality database entries.
We use a lightweight classifier to route simple greetings to a micro model, and complex coding questions to our flagship model.
We send every prompt to three different models simultaneously and pay for all of them to compare the answers.

exercises.explain: Routing queries dynamically based on complexity (sending simple ones to a cheap model and hard ones to a premium model) is a classic AI engineering pattern for cost optimization.

exercises.prompt: You are building a high-volume customer service chatbot. To achieve the lowest possible cost while maintaining high quality, which combined strategy should you implement?

options.text: Use the largest model for everything to ensure perfect accuracy.
Cache static system prompts, compress history, and route simple tasks to smaller models.
Disable system prompts entirely and send raw, uncompressed transcripts.
Avoid caching and compress only the final output text.

exercises.explain: By routing basic tasks to cheaper models, compressing long text histories to save on token processing, and caching static system prompts, you target all major cost drivers simultaneously without sacrificing quality.
